articles.title: FIFA World Cup: Friday schedule, predictions, standings, knockout race
articles.summary: World Cup knockout places are on the line as Groups G, H and I conclude, with fixtures, predictions, and standings.
articles.titleZh: FIFA 世界杯：周五赛程、预测、积分榜、淘汰赛
articles.summaryZh: 随着 G、H 和 I 组的结束，世界杯淘汰赛的席位、赛程、预测和积分榜即将揭晓。

contentParagraphs.en: The race for the World Cup knockout stage intensifies on Friday as the final Group G, H and I matches are played, with qualification, first place and several Round of 32 spots still up for grabs.France and Norway battle for top spot in Group I, Spain look to secure first place in Group H, while Egypt, Iran, Belgium, Cape Verde and Saudi Arabia are all fighting to extend their tournaments.Away from the action on the pitch, Mexico have completed a perfect group stage, Dutch fans have turned Kansas City into a sea of orange, African teams are chasing a historic knockout showing, and heartwarming fan moments continue to capture the spirit of the tournament.Here are the latest fixtures, predictions, standings and talking points from the FIFA World Cup 2026.What is the schedule for Friday? Norway vs France at Boston Stadium in the United States at 3pm EDT (19:00 GMT) Senegal vs Iraq at Toronto Stadium, in Canada at 3pm EDT (19:00 GMT) Cape Verde vs Saudi Arabia at Houston Stadium, in the US at 7pm CDT (00:00 GMT on Saturday) Uruguay vs Spain at Estadio Guadalajara in Mexico at 6pm CST (00:00 GMT on Saturday) Egypt vs Iran at Seattle Stadium in the US at 8pm PDT (03:00 GMT on Saturday) New Zealand vs Belgium at BC Place in Vancouver, Canada at 8pm PDT (03:00 GMT on Saturday) What are the predictions for Norway vs France?Norway and France meet for the first time since France’s 4-0 friendly victory in 2014, in what will be the 16th meeting between the two nations.Norway have won just two of their previous competitive meetings with France, with their last victory coming in a European Championship qualifier in 1987. They are also still searching for their first-ever World Cup win over European opposition, having drawn two and lost three of their previous five such matches.France, meanwhile, have won their last five World Cup matches against European teams.Opta’s supercomputer calculates France as the favourite, giving Les Bleus a 59.4 percent chance of victory.A draw is rated at 20.6 percent, enough for France to finish top of Group I, while Norway is given a 20 percent chance of claiming the three points, according to Opta.What are the predictions for Senegal vs Iraq?This is the first World Cup meeting between Senegal and Iraq. Senegal are unbeaten against AFC opposition at the tournament, drawing with Japan in 2018 and beating Qatar in 2022. Iraq have never faced an African team at a World Cup.Opta’s supercomputer makes Senegal the clear favourites, giving them a 77.2 percent chance of victory compared with 8.6 percent for Iraq, while the draw is rated at 14.2 percent.Senegal can no longer top Group I but still have a 72.2 percent chance of reaching the last 32. Iraq’s hopes are slim, with Opta giving them just a 1.1 percent chance of progressing.What are the predictions for Cape Verde vs Saudi Arabia?This will be the first World Cup meeting between Cape Verde and Saudi Arabia. Saudi Arabia have lost just one of their five previous World Cup matches against African opposition, winning two and drawing two.Opta’s supercomputer gives Cape Verde a slight edge, with a 40.8 percent chance of victory compared with 33.9 percent for Saudi Arabia. The draw is rated at 25.3 percent.Cape Verde have a 66.7 percent chance of reaching the last 32, while Saudi Arabia’s hopes stand at 33.3 percent.What are the predictions for Uruguay vs Spain?Former tournament winners Uruguay and Spain clash at the World Cup for the third time, but for the first time in more than three decades.Their two previous World Cup meetings both ended in draws: A 2-2 result in the final round of the 1950 tournament and a goalless group-stage deadlock at Italia ’90.Opta’s supercomputer makes Spain the clear favourites. The reigning European champions won 62.4 percent of 25,000 pre-match simulations, while Uruguay were victorious in 15.7 percent. A draw occurred in 21.9 percent of the simulations.What are the predictions for Egypt vs Iran?Egypt and Iran will meet for the first time at the World Cup. Their only previous meeting came at the 2000 LG Cup in Tehran, ending 1-1 before Egypt won 8-7 on penalties. Current Egypt coach Hossam Hassan scored, while Iran legend Ali Daei equalised.Iran are unbeaten against African opposition at the World Cup, beating Morocco in 2018 and drawing with Angola (2006) and Nigeria (2014).Opta’s supercomputer gives Egypt a slight edge, with a 42.9 percent chance of victory. A draw is rated at 32.2 percent, while Iran have a 24.9 percent chance of winning.What are the predictions for New Zealand vs Belgium?This is the first-ever meeting between New Zealand and Belgium. New Zealand are unbeaten in their last two World Cup matches against European opposition, drawing with Slovakia and Italy at the 2010 tournament.Belgium could become the first European side since their own team in 1998 to draw all three group-stage matches at a World Cup.Prediction: Opta’s supercomputer makes Belgium overwhelming favourites, giving them an 80.3 percent chance of victory. A draw is rated at 11.8 percent, while New Zealand prevails in just 7.9 percent of simulations.What do we know about the World Cup standings?As of Friday, June 26, six groups have completed play, while Groups G-L are still deciding the remaining Round of 32 places.Some key takeaways: Perfect group winners: Mexico (9 pts) are the only team with a perfect record. Already through: Mexico, South Africa, Switzerland, Canada, Bosnia and Herzegovina, Brazil, Morocco, USA, Australia, Germany, Ivory Coast, Ecuador, Netherlands, Japan, Sweden, France and Norway have all secured knockout places. Groups still to be decided today: Group G: Egypt lead on 4 points, with Iran and Belgium on 2 and New Zealand on 1. Group H: Spain lead with 4 points, while Uruguay and Cape Verde have 2 each. Group I: France and Norway have already qualified, but first place is still up for grabs. Groups J, K and L will conclude on Saturday. With the final group-stage matches still to be played, 13 p
contentParagraphs.zh: 周五，世界杯淘汰赛的争夺愈演愈烈，G、H、I组最后一场比赛即将打响，资格赛、第一名和多个32强席位仍有待争夺。法国和挪威在I组争夺头名，西班牙希望确保H组第一，而埃及、伊朗、比利时、佛得角和沙特阿拉伯都在为延长赛事而奋斗。抛开球场上的表现，墨西哥已经完成了完美的小组赛，荷兰球迷已经把堪萨斯城变成了一个足球场。在橙色的海洋中，非洲球队正在追逐历史性的淘汰赛表现，暖心的球迷时刻继续体现着赛事的精神。以下是 2026 年 FIFA 世界杯的最新赛程、预测、积分榜和谈话要点。周五的赛程安排是什么？挪威 vs 法国，美国东部时间下午 3 点（格林威治标准时间 19:00）在美国波士顿体育场举行；塞内加尔 vs 伊拉克，加拿大东部时间下午 3 点（格林威治标准时间 19:00）在加拿大多伦多体育场举行；佛得角 vs 沙特阿拉伯，美国中部时间晚上 7 点（格林威治标准时间周六 00:00）在美国休斯敦体育场对阵乌拉圭 vs 西班牙，中部标准时间下午 6 点（格林威治标准时间周六 00:00）在墨西哥瓜达拉哈拉体育场举行；埃及 vs 埃及伊朗于美国太平洋夏令时间晚上 8 点（格林威治标准时间周六 03:00）在美国西雅图体育场对阵新西兰对阵比利时，太平洋夏令时间晚上 8 点（格林威治标准时间周六 03:00）在加拿大温哥华 BC Place 对阵新西兰对比利时。挪威对阵法国的预测是什么？自 2014 年法国 4-0 友谊赛获胜以来，挪威和法国首次交锋，这将是两国之间的第 16 次交锋。挪威在之前的正式比赛中只赢了两次法国队上一次胜利是在 1987 年的欧洲杯预选赛上。他们还在寻求在世界杯上首次战胜欧洲对手，此前五场此类比赛中，他们取得了 2 平 3 负的成绩。与此同时，法国队在过去 5 场对阵欧洲球队的世界杯比赛中都取得了胜利。Opta 的超级计算机计算出法国队是夺冠热门，给了法国队 59.4% 的获胜机会。平局率为 20.6%，足以让法国队完赛根据 Opta 的数据，挪威队在 I 组中排名第一，而挪威队获得三分的机会为 20%。塞内加尔队对阵伊拉克队的预测是什么？这是塞内加尔队和伊拉克队的首次世界杯交锋。塞内加尔在本届世界杯上对亚足联对手保持不败，2018年战平日本，2022年击败卡塔尔。伊拉克从未在世界杯上遇到过非洲球队。Opta的超级计算机使塞内加尔成为明显的热门，他们的获胜几率为77.2%，而伊拉克的获胜几率为8.6%，而平局率为14.2%。塞内加尔不再是第一组第一，但仍拥有72.2的胜率进入最后 32 强的机会。伊拉克的希望很渺茫，Opta 给他们的晋级机会只有 1.1%。佛得角对阵沙特阿拉伯的预测是什么？这将是佛得角和沙特阿拉伯之间的第一次世界杯比赛。沙特阿拉伯在之前的五场世界杯对阵非洲对手的比赛中只输了一场，赢了两场，平了两场。Opta 的超级计算机让佛得角稍占优势，获胜的几率为 40.8%，而沙特阿拉伯的获胜几率为 33.9%。抽签率为25.3%。佛得角进入32强的几率为66.7%，而沙特阿拉伯的希望为33.3%。乌拉圭对阵西班牙的预测是什么？前世界杯冠军乌拉圭和西班牙将第三次在世界杯上交锋，但这是三十多年来的第一次。他们之前的两次世界杯交锋均以平局告终：最后一轮以2-2的比分结束。 1950 年的世界杯和意大利 '90 世界杯小组赛陷入僵局。Opta 的超级计算机使西班牙成为明显的热门。在 25,000 次赛前模拟中，卫冕欧洲冠军队的胜率为 62.4%，而乌拉圭队的胜率为 15.7%。 21.9% 的模拟中出现平局。埃及 vs 伊朗的预测是什么？埃及和伊朗将在世界杯上首次相遇。他们之前唯一一次交锋是在2000年德黑兰举行的LG杯上，最终以1-1告终，随后埃及队在点球大战中以8-7获胜。现任埃及主帅霍萨姆·哈桑进球，伊朗传奇人物阿里·代伊扳平比分。伊朗在世界杯上对非洲对手保持不败，2018 年击败摩洛哥，战平安哥拉（2006 年）和尼日利亚（2014 年）。Opta 的超级计算机让埃及稍占优势，获胜机会为 42.9%。平局概率为 32.2%，伊朗获胜概率为 24.9%。新西兰 vs 比利时的预测是什么？这是新西兰和比利时之间的首次交锋。新西兰在最近两场世界杯对阵欧洲对手的比赛中保持不败，在 2010 年世界杯上分别战平斯洛伐克和意大利。比利时可能成为自 1998 年自家球队以来第一支在世界杯小组赛三场比赛中全部打平的欧洲球队。预测：Opta 的超级计算机使比利时成为压倒性热门，让他们有 80.3% 的获胜机会。平局率为 11.8%，而新西兰在模拟中的胜率仅为 7.9%。我们对世界杯积分榜了解多少？截至 6 月 26 日星期五，6 个小组已经完成比赛，而 G-L 组仍在决定剩余的 32 个席位。一些关键要点： 完美小组冠军：墨西哥（9 分）是唯一一支拥有完美战绩的球队。已经晋级的球队：墨西哥、南非、瑞士、加拿大、波斯尼亚和黑塞哥维那、巴西、摩洛哥、美国、澳大利亚、德国、科特迪瓦、厄瓜多尔、荷兰、日本、瑞典、法国和挪威均已锁定淘汰赛席位。今天仍待决出小组：G组：埃及积4分领先，伊朗和比利时积2分，新西兰积1分。H组：西班牙积4分领先，乌拉圭和佛得角各积2分。第一组：法国队和挪威队已经出线，但第一名仍悬而未决。 J、K 和 L 组将于周六结束。由于小组赛最后一场比赛尚未结束，13点

articles.title: Teens who hacked TfL were known to police years before cyber-attack
articles.summary: Owen Flowers and Thalha Jubair were convicted for their roles in the attack, which led to large costs for Transport for London.
articles.titleZh: 警方在网络攻击发生前几年就知道黑客入侵伦敦交通局的青少年
articles.summaryZh: 欧文·弗劳尔斯（Owen Flowers）和塔哈·朱贝尔（Thalha Jubair）因参与袭击而被定罪，这给伦敦交通局带来了巨额损失。

contentParagraphs.en: Flowers and Jubair's trial heard they were part of the cyber-crime collective, Scattered Spider.The loosely organised gang of young English-speaking cyber-criminals has been linked to dozens of other cyber-attacks including on retailers Marks and Spencer and the Co-op.But the BBC has learned Flowers initially came to the attention of police shortly after he turned 16 years old.In October 2023 he was caught carrying out low-level cyber-crime and visited by West Midland's Regional Cyber Crime Unit prevent officers.Police say that during the visit Flowers did not engage with officers and was given a cease and desist order to deter him from further offending.Police had the option to invite him to enrol in the national Cyber Choices programme, which works to steer young people away from cyber-crime. However Flowers was already being investigated for an offence and was reluctant to engage with officers, so they deemed him not suitable.Just months later, the teenager - who was living with his grandmother - went on to commit a series of increasingly serious cyber-offences with Scattered Spider which culminated in the TfL attack.NCA deputy director Paul Foster, head of its National Cyber Crime Unit, said the case highlighted the challenges posed by a small number of highly capable offenders.He called for stronger legal powers - such as the proposed Cyber Crime Risk Orders (CCROs) - to deal with cases like this.CCROs, announced by the UK government as part of planned reforms to the Computer Misuse Act, are designed to let police and courts place restrictions on people considered high risk before they carry out further serious breaches. They would "enable earlier law enforcement interventions against high-risk cyber-crime offenders," Foster said.Flowers was eventually arrested on 16 September 2024 in connection with the TfL attack, which had started on 31 August.In the arrest raid, investigators seized multiple devices from his bedroom, including laptops, desktop computers, hard drives and USB storage devices. They reportedly discovered cryptocurrency holdings worth millions of pounds.During the investigation, NCA officers uncovered evidence that computer systems belonging to two US healthcare organisations, SSM Health and Sutter Health, had also been infiltrated and damaged. Flowers later pleaded guilty to offences relating to those hacks. He is wanted in the US but the BBC understands authorities there will not be persuing further action against him.After being charged, Flowers was released on bail under strict conditions. He breached those conditions twice, in March 2025 and May 2025.His co-defendant Jubair had also been known to police for years.In 2023, while still a juvenile, he received a Youth Rehabilitation Order for cyber offences linked to the Lapsus$ hacking group, which targeted major companies including Nvidia and BT/EE. Because he was under 18, his identity could not be reported at the time.Jubair has 22 previous convictions in total and began offending at 14 years old. He is also wanted in the US in connection with cyber-crimes that allegedly stole and extorted $87m (£66.1m) from victims.
contentParagraphs.zh: Flowers 和 Jubair 的审判听说他们是网络犯罪集团“分散蜘蛛”的成员。这个组织松散的年轻英语网络犯罪团伙与数十起其他网络攻击有关，其中包括针对零售商 Marks and Spencer 和 Co-op 的网络攻击。但 BBC 获悉，Flowers 最初在 16 岁后不久就引起了警方的注意。2023 年 10 月，他因进行低级网络犯罪而被捕，并访问了西米德兰地区网络犯罪部门阻止警察。警方表示，在访问期间，弗劳尔斯没有与警察接触，并收到了停止令，以阻止他进一步犯罪。警方可以选择邀请他参加国家网络选择计划，该计划致力于引导年轻人远离网络犯罪。然而，弗劳尔斯已经因犯罪行为而接受调查，并且不愿意与警方接触，因此他们认为他不适合。几个月后，这名与祖母住在一起的少年继续与分散的蜘蛛犯下一系列日益严重的网络犯罪，最终导致了伦敦交通局的攻击。NCA 副主任、国家网络犯罪部门负责人保罗·福斯特表示，此案突显了少数高素质犯罪者所带来的挑战。他呼吁加强法律权力，例如提议的网络犯罪风险令 (CCRO) - 处理此类案件。CCRO 是英国政府作为《计算机滥用法》计划改革的一部分而宣布的，旨在让警察和法院在被视为高风险的人实施进一步的严重违规行为之前对其进行限制。福斯特说，它们将“让执法部门能够更早地对高风险网络犯罪者进行干预”。弗劳尔斯最终于 2024 年 9 月 16 日因与 8 月 31 日开始的伦敦交通局攻击有关而被捕。在逮捕行动中，调查人员从他的卧室查获了多台设备，包括笔记本电脑、台式电脑、硬盘和 USB 存储设备。据报道，他们发现了价值数百万英镑的加密货币。在调查过程中，NCA 官员发现了证据，表明属于美国两家医疗机构 SSM Health 和 Sutter Health 的计算机系统也遭到渗透和损坏。弗劳尔斯后来承认了与这些黑客攻击有关的罪行。他在美国受到通缉，但 BBC 了解到当局不会对他采取进一步行动。在受到指控后，弗劳尔斯在严格条件下获得保释。他在 2025 年 3 月和 2025 年 5 月两次违反了这些条件。他的同案被告 Jubair 也已为警方所知多年。2023 年，当他还是青少年时，他因与 Lapsus$ 黑客组织有关的网络犯罪而收到了青少年改造令，该组织的目标包括 Nvidia 和 BT/EE 等大公司。由于他未满 18 岁，当时无法透露他的身份。 Jubair 总共有 22 次犯罪前科，从 14 岁开始犯罪。他还因涉嫌从受害者那里窃取和勒索 8700 万美元（6610 万英镑）的网络犯罪而在美国受到通缉。

articles.title: I'm back at home again after uni - here's how I'm making it work
articles.summary: Rising costs are forcing more young adults to live with their parents, here's how not to come to blows.
articles.titleZh: 大学毕业后我再次回到家 - 这就是我如何让它发挥作用
articles.summaryZh: 不断上涨的生活成本迫使更多的年轻人与父母住在一起，以下是如何避免打架的方法。

contentParagraphs.en: When 24-year-old Natasha Suman moved back into her parents' home in Bedford after university, she only expected it to be for a "few months" while she searched for her first job. But almost three years later, she's still there, saving for a deposit on her first home.The marketing coordinator pays towards bills but doesn't pay rent, so is able to put aside £1,000 a month in a Lifetime ISA - a tax-free account topped up by the government. That would have been impossible had she lived by herself, she says, given the "cost of living".However, Natasha admits she has "less freedom" than when she lived independently and generally does fewer "spontaneous things".She also argues more with her family than she used to, despite feeling very fortunate to live with them."When I left home [for university], I was a very different person, and by the time I came back, I had essentially become an adult," she says."Because of that, there have definitely been some clashes between me and my parents."The proportion of people in their 20s and 30s living with their parents has increased sharply over the last three decades, as rising house prices and rents have forced many to move back in to save for their first home. But while it can be a practical way to save money or deal with the loss of a job or a relationship breakdown, it often comes with frustrations, such as feeling like you've lost your independence or even regressed to childhood.For Natasha and her parents, Rita and Pawan, flashpoints have included shared use of the family car after her own vehicle broke down, disagreements over how chores are divided, and how much time to spend together - with her parents wanting to see more of her."It has been an adjustment for all of us."A lot of these disagreements stem from the fact that we are now four adults living together, all with our own routines, expectations and opinions."Problems have been avoided by having conversations early on, she says. Her parents set "clear expectations" such as cleaning up after herself and making her own lunch.The family has also discussed privacy, with Natasha asking her parents to knock before coming into her room."I tend to spend more time in my room to relax and unwind than I did before. Initially, my parents did not really understand this, but after talking about it, they have become more understanding."Caroline Bentham, 37, who has lived with her mother Mary in Yorkshire for nearly seven years, got in touch with BBC Your Voice. She says the experience has been really positive - although she "never imagined this would be me in my 30s".She split from her partner in 2019 and was only supposed to live with her mum for six to 12 months while she started her PhD. But then the pandemic hit, along with various other life events, and she says it "kept making sense" to stay.The transition to living together again was a "real challenge" at first, she says, as her mum struggled to give up control in areas like the kitchen. They also had "lots of arguments" as they worked out "how to be around each other". "It might sound cliché but we had to learn a new way of communicating," she says. One of the biggest benefits of living with her mother is the emotional support they give each other, Caroline says. But she admits the arrangement is sometimes not great for her self-esteem and there is "definitely a stigma about living with parents".Tips for adults who live with their parentsAgree practical expectations around finances, chores, visitors, quiet times and shared spacesRecognise that living at home does not mean reverting to dependence and contribute where you can, financially and/or in terms of houseworkDon't assume old family roles still apply: what worked when you were 16 is unlikely to work when you are 36Source: RelateChristodoulidi says one of the overlooked advantages of living as an adult with a parent is the chance to know each other differently."Parents often begin to see their child as another adult, while adult children gain a fuller understanding of their parents as people rather than simply as parents."She also says society needs to ditch the stereotype that adult children who still live at home have "failed to launch". Natasha says it helps to remind herself that living with her family is a "temporary" situation that will "lead to a better outcome in the future". The extra time she gets to spend with her parents is a "blessing", she adds. "One day I'll move out, get married and have my own family, and I won't have as much time with them," she says.
contentParagraphs.zh: 当 24 岁的娜塔莎·苏曼 (Natasha Suman) 大学毕业后搬回父母位于贝德福德的家中时，她只希望在寻找第一份工作时能呆“几个月”。但差不多三年后，她仍然留在那里，为她的第一套房子存钱。营销协调员支付账单，但不支付租金，因此每月可以在终身 ISA 中存入 1,000 英镑——一个由政府充值的免税账户。她说，考虑到“生活成本”，如果她独自生活，这是不可能的。然而，娜塔莎承认，与独立生活时相比，她的“自由度更少”，而且通常做的“自发的事情”也更少。她与家人的争吵也比以前更多，尽管她觉得和家人住在一起很幸运。“当我离开家（上大学）时，我是一个非常不同的人，当我回来时，我基本上已经成为一个成年人了，”她说。“因为“在过去的三十年里，随着房价和租金的上涨，迫使许多人搬回家乡，为他们的第一套房子存钱，与父母住在一起的20多岁和30多岁的人的比例急剧增加。虽然这可能是一种省钱、应对失业或关系破裂的实用方法，但它常常会带来挫败感，比如感觉自己失去了独立性，甚至倒退到了童年。对于娜塔莎和她的父母丽塔和帕万来说，闪点包括在她自己的车辆抛锚后共用家庭汽车，在如何分配家务上存在分歧，以及花多少时间在一起 - 她的父母希望更多地见到她。 “很多分歧源于这样一个事实：我们现在是四个成年人住在一起，都有自己的日常生活、期望和观点。”她说，她的父母通过尽早交谈就避免了问题，比如自己清理干净，自己做午餐。一家人还讨论了隐私问题，娜塔莎要求父母在进入她的房间之前敲门。“我倾向于花更多的时间在我的房间里放松和放松。起初，我的父母并不太理解这一点，但经过交谈后，他们变得更加理解了。”37 岁的卡罗琳·边沁 (Caroline Bentham) 与母亲玛丽在约克郡生活了近七年，她与 BBC Your Voice 取得了联系。她说，这段经历非常积极，尽管她“从未想过这会是 30 多岁的我”。她于 2019 年与伴侣分手，开始时只应与母亲住在一起 6 到 12 个月。但后来疫情爆发，加上其他生活事件，她说继续住下去“仍然有意义”。她说，重新住在一起是一个“真正的挑战”，因为她的妈妈在考虑“如何相处”时也发生了“很多争吵”，“这可能听起来很陈词滥调，但我们必须学习一种新的沟通方式，”她说，这是与母亲住在一起的最大好处之一。卡罗琳说，“这是他们给予彼此的情感支持”，但她承认，这种安排有时不利于她的自尊，而且“与父母住在一起肯定是一种耻辱”。给与父母住在一起的成年人的建议就财务、家务、访客、安静时间和共享空间达成实际期望。认识到住在家里并不意味着恢复依赖，并在经济上和/或家务方面尽你所能做出贡献。不要认为旧的家庭角色仍然适用：有效的方法。 16岁的时候不太可能在36岁的时候工作 来源：RelateChristodoulidi说，作为一个成年人与父母一起生活的一个被忽视的好处是有机会以不同的方式了解彼此。“父母经常开始将他们的孩子视为另一个成年人，而成年子女则更全面地了解他们的父母作为人，而不仅仅是父母。”她还表示，社会需要抛弃仍然住在家里的成年子女“未能启动”的刻板印象。她补充说，提醒自己，与家人住在一起是一种“暂时”的情况，这将“为她带来更好的未来”，她补充道，“有一天我会搬出去，结婚并拥有自己的家庭，但我不会有那么多时间和他们在一起。”

articles.title: UK sees hottest June day on record as 36.1C recorded in Hampshire
articles.summary: Heat records were broken when temperatures soared across southern England on Wednesday afternoon.
articles.titleZh: 英国汉普郡迎来有记录以来最热 6 月气温 36.1 摄氏度
articles.summaryZh: 周三下午，英格兰南部气温飙升，打破了高温记录。

contentParagraphs.en: Figure caption, 'No let up' - The people working in extreme heatByElla Kipling and Anna LamcheUpdated 7 hours agoThe UK has experienced its hottest June day on record after temperatures soared to 36.1C (97F) in Gosport, Hampshire, on Wednesday afternoon.Hundreds of schools shut across England and Wales and transport has also been disrupted, with train passengers advised to avoid all non-essential travel.The heatwave is forecast to continue into Friday, with a further high of about 38C possible, the Met Office said.A red extreme heat warning issued by the Met Office across parts of south and central England and south Wales remains in place until 23:59 BST on Thursday.Wednesday's high came between 15:00 and 16:00, breaking the previous June record of 35.6C recorded in Southampton in 1976 and Camden in 1957.This new record is described as "provisional" by the Met Office, which now has to conduct checks to ensure the measurement is reliable.The next highest temperatures of the day were all recorded in southern England, including Wisley, Surrey, on 36C, Wiggonholt, West Sussex, on 35.9C, and Charlwood, Surrey, on 35.7C. And Wales had its hottest day of the year so far, with a high of 33.3C at Cardiff Bute Park.A searing European heatwave continues to cause deaths and disruption, with France recording its hottest day since records began in 1947, and the temperature in Paris and other areas above 40C.Among the areas covered by the UK red warning are the East Midlands, the east of England, London and south-east England, south-west England, Wales and the West Midlands.These regions can expect an "exceptional spell of hot and humid weather" with impacts to the general population "highly likely", according to the Met Office. It is only the second time a red warning has been issued since extreme heat warnings began in 2021.Climate change has led to more frequent and intense heatwaves, said the Met Office's chief scientist, Professor Stephen Belcher."To see temperatures like this in the UK in June is sobering," he added.Image source, EPAImage caption, June temperature records set in 1976 and 1957 were brokenA wider amber warning covers much of England and Wales through Thursday,, external with areas as far north as Manchester expected to see temperatures reach above 30C.A separate amber warning for extreme heat, lasting from midnight on Friday, external until 23:59 covers south-east England, eastern England and parts of the Midlands. And a new amber weather warning has been issued, external for between 00:00 and 21:00 on Saturday, covering parts of southern and eastern England.The daily air temperature record for the UK in June is 35.6C, which was reached on 28 June 1976 - but this is very likely to be exceeded in the coming days.The "feels like" temperature, which indicates how people will experience the heat, is likely to reach more than 40C in parts of England. Matthew Lehnert, chief forecaster at the Met Office, said high humidity meant the heat would feel "much more potent".Parts of the UK are also set to experience "tropical nights", which occur when night-time temperatures stay above 20C.The Met Office has warned that the weather could result in "population-wide adverse health effects", leading to serious illness or danger to life.Changes in working practices will be required, and there will be "significantly more" people visiting coastal areas and lakes, leading to an increased risk of water safety incidents.It comes as a major rescue operation was initiated in Hampshire after a 15-year-old boy went missing while swimming in a nature reserve lake.Several people stranded in traffic on the M25 near Godstone in Surrey after a crash are being treated for heat-related illnesses, South East Coast Ambulance Service said.Train operators including South Western Railway, Thameslink, Northern and Avanti West Coast have asked people to make only essential journeys and have put reduced timetables in place. Transport for London warned passengers that "very high temperatures“ might cause disruption to some Tube and rail services.Image source, PAImage caption, King Charles got a helping hand to stay cool when he attended a London Climate Week receptionImage source, BBC NewsbeatImage caption, Ice cream man Emery, 17, said customers were coming out on "almost every street" because of the heatThe heat has affected working people across the country. Telecoms engineer Jake Bird told the BBC how he was preparing for a week of high temperatures in his job, which requires him to climb telephone poles in PPE and a harness.The 21-year-old has decided to keep working as he is self-employed but said he would take strategic cooling breaks.The engineer, from Oxfordshire, said: "Here it's going to be 36 degrees and that is just insane, especially going up a pole in all that kit and everything. It's just going to be absolutely dreadful."Bricklayer Elijah told BBC Newsbeat he tried to "keep the complaining [about the heat] to a minimum because about 10 months of the year, you're in rain and the cold".But he admitted the heat was a "lot to deal with", so his team in London had been starting work earlier than normal and finishing by midday. He also gives his employees water and ice lollies to keep them cool.For 17-year-old ice cream man Emery, from Gloucester, the heat is "good for money and for the business". However, the extreme heat also brings costs: everything in the van has to be plugged in overnight to keep it cool for the next day.The UK Health Security Agency (UKHSA) has also issued a red heat-health alert, which remains in place until 23:00 on Thursday.Network Rail is advising passengers travelling to, from or within the current red weather warning zone to make only necessary journeys on Thursday and Friday.Dr Agostinho Sousa, head of extreme events and health protection at UKHSA, said it was "vitally important" for people to understand the risk the high temperatures posed."A red heat health alert indicates a risk t

articles.title: Outrage as woman jailed for three years after criticising Somali government online
articles.summary:
Sentencing of 27-year-old Sadia Moalim Ali condemned by former president and prime ministers as well as rights groups
A rickshaw driver in Somalia has been sentenced to three years in prison for comments she made on social media, in a case that has caught the public’s attention a
articles.titleZh: 女子在网上批评索马里政府后被判入狱三年，引发愤怒
articles.summaryZh:
27 岁萨迪亚·莫阿利姆·阿里的判决受到前总统、总理以及人权组织的谴责
索马里一名人力车司机因在社交媒体上发表言论而被判处三年监禁，此案引起公众关注

contentParagraphs.en: A rickshaw driver in Somalia has been sentenced to three years in prison for comments she made on social media, in a case that has caught the public’s attention and provoked outrage in the country.Sadia Moalim Ali, a 27-year-old nursing graduate, was originally charged with insulting government institutions and incitement to commit a crime, but convicted only of the former. Her sentence, immediately condemned as “fundamentally unjust”, was handed down on 25 June.She had made critical comments on Facebook and TikTok about the federal government of Somalia, speaking out about the youth unemployment and high fuel prices gripping the country, as well as against alleged corruption, nepotism and forced evictions.The sentencing of the young mother has been condemned by former high-ranking government officials, including the former president of Somalia, Sharif Sheikh Ahmed, and human rights organisations.Hassan Ali Khaire, former prime minister of Somalia, wrote on X: “The three-year prison sentence handed down today … is deeply troubling and fundamentally unjust. This politically motivated arrest and conviction … reflects a disturbing pattern of judicial overreach, political retaliation, and abuse of state authority.”In a statement, the Coalition of Somali Human Rights Defenders called for her immediate release, saying: “Ali’s conviction and harsh sentence represent a serious attack on freedom of expression and the legitimate work of human rights defenders in Somalia.”It said her case reflected a broader pattern of systematic discrimination against women who advocated for social and political change. “Female human rights defenders in Somalia continue to face disproportionate risks,” it said, “including arbitrary arrest, judicial harassment, intimidation, online abuse and gender-based discrimination, aimed at excluding them from civic and political participation.”Somali media reported that Mohamed Sheikh Osman, one of Ali’s lawyers, rejected the ruling and said the defence would appeal.“[Ali] is not satisfied with the decision of the Banaadir Regional Court,” he told reporters. “The court issued a harsh ruling that could have been avoided.”Ali, who is the main breadwinner for her family and has a one-year-old daughter, has been in custody since 12 April. In an interview from prison in May, she told the Guardian she had been subjected to torture. She said: “I was forced to lie face down on the ground, and water was poured on me. I was kicked by guards with boots on. They stood over me and beat me with a baton.“I was taken into solitary confinement and kept there for two days. I was deprived of food and basic necessities while I was locked in that cell. I wasn’t allowed to leave to go to the toilet.”At court, Ali said police officers had threatened her with rape.Torture – defined as any act by which severe pain or suffering, physical or mental, is intentionally inflicted – is prohibited under international law and by the UN Convention against Torture in all circumstances.Since 2022, Somali authorities have been accused of engaging in a systematic and escalating crackdown on human rights, using arbitrary arrests, detention, harassment, threats and intimidation to silence journalists, activists and other people who express dissenting views.
contentParagraphs.zh: 索马里一名人力车司机因在社交媒体上发表言论而被判处三年监禁，该案件引起公众关注并在该国激起公愤。 27岁的护理毕业生萨迪亚·莫阿利姆·阿里最初被指控侮辱政府机构和煽动犯罪，但仅因前者被定罪。她的判决于 6 月 25 日下达，立即被谴责为“根本不公正”。她曾在 Facebook 和 TikTok 上发表了对索马里联邦政府的批评，谈到困扰该国的年轻人失业和高油价，以及反对所谓的腐败、裙带关系和强迫驱逐。对这位年轻母亲的判决受到了前高级政府官员的谴责，包括索马里前总统谢里夫·谢赫·艾哈迈德和人类。索马里前总理哈桑·阿里·海尔在 X 上写道：“今天判处的三年监禁……令人深感不安，而且从根本上来说是不公正的。这种出于政治动机的逮捕和定罪……反映了司法越权、政治报复和滥用国家权力的令人不安的模式。”索马里人权捍卫者联盟在一份声明中呼吁立即释放她，并表示：“阿里的定罪和严厉判决是对自由的严重攻击索马里人权捍卫者的言论和合法工作。”报告称，她的案件反映了对主张社会和政治变革的妇女的更广泛的系统性歧视模式。报告称：“索马里的女性人权维护者继续面临不成比例的风险，包括任意逮捕、司法骚扰、恐吓、网络虐待和性别歧视，旨在将她们排除在公民和政治参与之外。”索马里媒体报道称，阿里的律师之一穆罕默德·谢赫·奥斯曼拒绝了这一裁决，并表示辩方将上诉。“[阿里]对巴纳迪尔地区法院的裁决并不满意”法院，”他告诉记者。 “法院做出了本可以避免的严厉裁决。” 阿里是家庭的主要经济支柱，有一个一岁的女儿，自 4 月 12 日起一直被拘留。五月份在监狱接受采访时，她告诉卫报她曾遭受酷刑。她说：“我被迫面朝下趴在地上，水浇在我身上。我被穿靴子的恶警踢。他们站在我身上，用警棍殴打我。“我被单独监禁了两天。当我被锁在牢房里时，我被剥夺了食物和基本必需品。我不被允许离开去厕所。”阿里在法庭上说，警察威胁要强奸她。酷刑——定义为故意造成身体或精神上的严重疼痛或痛苦的任何行为——在任何情况下都是国际法和《联合国禁止酷刑公约》所禁止的。自 2022 年以来，索马里当局被指控对人权进行系统性和不断升级的镇压，使用任意逮捕、拘留、骚扰、威胁和恐吓记者、活动人士和其他表达不同意见的人。

articles.title: Apple hikes some prices by nearly 20% while Xbox raises console cost
articles.summary: Apple said it had "never seen a component price increase this much, this quickly".
articles.titleZh: 苹果将​​部分产品价格提高近 20%，而 Xbox 则提高了主机成本
articles.summaryZh: 苹果表示，“从未见过零部件价格上涨如此之快”。

contentParagraphs.en: Apple is increasing the price of MacBooks and iPads worldwide due to rising memory and storage chip costs.The iPhone maker has hiked the prices of some laptops and tablets by almost 20%, saying the electronics industry is facing an "unprecedented challenge" due to an "extraordinary surge" in demand for chips to power AI data centres."We have never seen a component price increase this much, this quickly," the company said - adding it was working to find solutions.Not long after Apple's announcement, Xbox said it had decided to significantly raise the price of its popular gaming console for the second time in less than a year due to the current "components crisis".The Microsoft-owned company said on Thursday that the price of its basic console will go up by $100 (£75) to $499, while the price of a console with more memory will go up by $150, to $749. New prices will take effect from August.Xbox previously hiked the price of its consoles in October by $20-$70, meaning the price of a new console will be 30% to 40% more expensive than it was this time last year.The company said it had "hoped another price increase would not be necessary", but blamed the rising cost of console storage and memory for needing to raise prices on consumers."The entire consumer electronics industry is struggling with the current components crisis, but the effects are particularly hard on consoles," Xbox said.The company added that while the cost of memory and storage has already more than doubled, it expects the costs to double again by 2027.That seemingly leaves the door open for Xbox to further raise prices.Apple's and Xbox's price hikes follow a slew of firms increasing device prices to help them absorb rising hardware costs.Commenting before the Xbox price increase was publicised, tech analyst Paolo Pescatore said Apple's price rises showed the "AI boom was now affecting consumer electronics".Much of the increased prices for memory and storage components - particularly Ram, a form of computer memory - have been attributed to a proliferation of data centres needed to power the AI boom.This, experts say, has caused an imbalance between supply and demand which means everyone has to pay more.The world's largest chipmaker, Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company (TSMC), has also said that inflation is pushing up the cost of doing business.Speaking to the BBC earlier in June, Wendell Huang of TSMC - which makes chips for the likes of Nvidia, AMD and Apple - did not rule out its own price rises amid spiking costs.Pescatore said Apple's actions demonstrated the extent of the challenges for "even for the world's biggest technology companies"."This is a significant moment because even Apple, with its scale and buying power, is no longer immune to the rising cost of key components," he told the BBC.Affected hardware included the MacBook Pro with 1 terabyte of storage, which rose to $1,999 from $1,699 on its US store.Meanwhile in the UK, the Neo - Apple's lowest-priced laptop - has increased from £599 to £699 within months of its launch."We have shielded our customers from these increases so far, but we have now reached a point where we need to begin raising prices on a number of products, including today's increases for iPad and Mac," the company said in its statement.David Naranjo of market research firm Counterpoint said he expected other PC and tablet brands would follow Apple by upping their costs."They may raise prices on select products, cut discounts on entry-level models, or adjust their product lines to focus more on premium devices," he said.Dipanjan Chatterjee, vice president and principal analyst at market research firm Forrester, said he believed Apple's loyal customer base would take the financial hit without too much outcry."If anyone can survive a price increase with minimal blowback, it's Apple," he added.Tim Cook, Apple's outgoing chief executive, had also hinted at the changes - telling the Wall Street Journal earlier in June that price increases were "unavoidable" due to the "unsustainable" situation around memory chips."We definitely need memory pricing and supply to return to reasonable levels for consumer products. That's the bottom line," he told the publication.The soaring costs have affected a wide variety of companies and products across the technology sector, including PCs and consoles.On Monday gaming giant Valve said its original goal for the price of its gaming PC the Steam Machine was "no longer viable", instead launching it at a price of £879 in the UK and $1,049 in the US.
contentParagraphs.zh: 由于内存和存储芯片成本上涨，苹果正在全球范围内提高 MacBook 和 iPad 的价格。这家 iPhone 制造商已将部分笔记本电脑和平板电脑的价格提高了近 20%，称由于人工智能数据中心供电芯片的需求“异常激增”，电子行业正面临“前所未有的挑战”。该公司表示，“我们从未见过零部件价格上涨得如此之快”，并补充说正在努力寻找解决方案。表示，由于当前的“零部件危机”，它决定在不到一年的时间内第二次大幅提高其受欢迎的游戏机的价格。这家微软旗下的公司周四表示，其基本游戏机的价格将上涨 100 美元（75 英镑），达到 499 美元，而具有更多内存的游戏机的价格将上涨 150 美元，达到 749 美元。新价格将从8月起生效。Xbox此前在10月份将其游戏机价格上调了20-70美元，这意味着新游戏机的价格将比去年同期贵30%至40%。该公司表示，“希望不需要再次涨价”，但将需要提高价格的责任归咎于消费者的游戏机存储和内存成本上升。“整个消费电子行业都在与当前的零部件危机作斗争，但其影响尤其严重该公司补充说，虽然内存和存储的成本已经增加了一倍以上，但预计到 2027 年成本将再次增加一倍。这似乎为 Xbox 进一步提价打开了大门。苹果和 Xbox 的价格上涨是在许多公司提高设备价格以帮助他们消化不断上涨的硬件成本之后进行的。技术分析师 Paolo Pescatore 在 Xbox 提价公布之前发表评论称，苹果的提价表明“人工智能热潮现在正在影响消费者”内存和存储组件（尤其是计算机内存的一种形式 RAM）价格上涨，很大程度上归因于推动人工智能繁荣所需的数据中心激增。专家表示，这导致了供需失衡，意味着每个人都必须支付更多费用。全球最大的芯片制造商台积电 (TSMC) 也表示，通货膨胀正在推高经营成本。 6 月初，台积电 (TSMC) 的 Wendell Huang 接受 BBC 采访。英伟达、AMD 和苹果公司的联合创始人 - 不排除在成本飙升的情况下自己涨价的可能性。佩斯卡托雷表示，苹果公司的行动表明了“即使是世界上最大的科技公司”所面临的挑战的程度。“这是一个重要的时刻，因为即使是苹果公司，以其规模和购买力，也不再能免受关键零部件成本上涨的影响，”他告诉 BBC。受影响的硬件包括具有 1 TB 存储空间的 MacBook Pro，其价格从 1,999 美元上涨到 1,999 美元。美国商店的售价为 1,699 美元。与此同时，在英国，Apple 价格最低的笔记本电脑 Neo 在推出后的几个月内就从 599 英镑涨至 699 英镑。该公司在声明中表示，“到目前为止，我们已经让客户免受这些涨价的影响，但我们现在已经到了需要开始提高多种产品价格的地步，包括今天的 iPad 和 Mac 涨价。”市场研究公司 Counterpoint 的 David Naranjo 表示，他预计其他 PC 和平板电脑品牌也会涨价。他说：“他们可能会提高部分产品的价格，降低入门级型号的折扣，或者调整产品线，更多地关注高端设备。”市场研究公司 Forrester 的副总裁兼首席分析师迪潘詹·查特吉 (Dipanjan Chatterjee) 表示，他相信苹果的忠实客户群会在没有太多抗议的情况下承受财务打击。也暗示了这些变化 - 六月初告诉《华尔街日报》，由于内存芯片的“不可持续”状况，价格上涨是“不可避免的”。“我们绝对需要内存定价和供应才能恢复到消费产品的合理水平。这是底线，”他告诉该出版物。成本飙升已经影响了整个技术领域的各种公司和产品，包括个人电脑和游戏机。周一，游戏巨头 Valve 表示，其游戏 PC Steam Machine 的最初价格目标“不再是”。可行”，而不是在英国以 879 英镑、在美国 1,049 美元的价格推出。

articles.title: Reeves backs Burnham to become next prime minister
articles.summary: The chancellor throws her support behind the MP for Makerfield despite reports he could demote her if he becomes PM.
articles.titleZh: 里夫斯支持伯纳姆成为下一任总理
articles.summaryZh: 尽管有报道称，如果梅克菲尔德成为总理，他可能会将她降职，但默克尔仍支持梅克菲尔德议员。

contentParagraphs.en: Rachel Reeves has backed Andy Burnham to be the next prime minister, despite reports he could demote her to a junior cabinet position if he becomes the Labour Party leader.Burnham, who was sworn in as the MP for Makerfield on Monday following his historic by-election victory, is expected to replace Reeves as chancellor if he enters No 10.Reeves told the British Chambers of Commerce (BCC) annual conference on Thursday that she did not want to pre-empt his choice of chancellor and urged her potential successor to stick to her fiscal rules.But she said she was proud of her record and that there was "unfinished business in fiscal devolution" - an area she said Burnham was also committed to.Reeves added that Burnham backed her rules - ensuring day-to-day spending is funded through tax revenue by the end of the Parliament, only borrowing for capital investment and reducing debt as a proportion of GDP."Andy has been really explicit - he backs those fiscal rules," she told the BCC conference.In an earlier interview with the BBC, Reeves said: "The last government lost control of the public finances."It sent inflation through the roof and interest rates through the roof. So control of the public finances has a real impact on families today."While Reeves said she had improved the flexibility in the government's budget by increasing its financial headroom, she added the public must be left in no doubt about the party's commitment to fiscal discipline."Because if you lose control of the public finances, it is ordinary working people and businesses who pay the price with higher inflation, higher taxes and indeed higher interest rates," she added.She told the BCC conference she and Burnham were also aligned on fiscal devolution - transferring financial powers like tax and spending from Westminster to local authorities - including, for example, on possible visitor levies on hotel bookings.Burnham is so far the only contender to replace Sir Keir Starmer, who announced his resignation earlier this week following a slew of senior cabinet resignations.If no other challenger emerges, Burnham could become prime minister as soon as 17 July.Asked by Sky News anchor Sophie Ridge at the conference whether she accepted there needed to be a change, Reeves said: "Clearly we need to do more to connect to the country to tell a better story of what we've done in government but also set out the vision of where we want the country to go."That is what Andy will be able to provide as our next prime minister. He is a great communicator, he's got a great track record of delivering in Greater Manchester, and I have no doubt he will bring that to the position of prime minister."
contentParagraphs.zh: 雷切尔·里夫斯(Rachel Reeves)支持安迪·伯纳姆(Andy Burnham)担任下一任首相，尽管有报道称，如果安迪·伯纳姆(Andy Burnham)成为工党领袖，他可能会将她降级为初级内阁职位。伯纳姆(Burnham)在历史性补选胜利后于周一宣誓就任梅克菲尔德议员，如果他进入第10位，预计将取代里夫斯成为财政大臣。里夫斯周四在英国商会(BCC)年会上表示，她不想抢占他的选择并敦促她的潜在继任者遵守她的财政规则。但她表示，她对自己的记录感到自豪，并且“财政权力下放方面还有未竟的事业”——她说伯纳姆也致力于这一领域。里夫斯补充说，伯纳姆支持她的规则——确保在议会结束前通过税收收入资助日常支出，只为资本投资借款并减少债务占GDP的比例。“安迪非常明确——他支持这些财政规则，”她告诉《金融时报》 BCC会议。在早些时候接受BBC采访时，里夫斯表示：“上届政府失去了对公共财政的控制。”它导致通货膨胀和利率飙升。因此，公共财政的控制对当今的家庭产生了真正的影响。“虽然里夫斯表示，她通过增加财政空间提高了政府预算的灵活性，但她补充说，公众必须对党对财政纪律的承诺毫无疑问。”她补充道，“因为如果失去对公共财政的控制，普通工薪阶层和企业就会付出更高的通货膨胀、更高的税收和更高的利率的代价。”她在 BCC 会议上表示，她和伯纳姆在财政问题上也保持一致。权力下放——将税收和支出等财政权力从威斯敏斯特移交给地方当局——包括，例如，可能对酒店预订征收游客税。伯纳姆是迄今为止取代基尔·斯塔默爵士的唯一竞争者，后者在一系列高级内阁辞职后于本周早些时候宣布辞职。如果没有其他挑战者出现，伯纳姆最早可能在 7 月 17 日成为首相。天空新闻主播索菲·里奇在会议上被问到她是否接受这一职位里夫斯说：“显然，我们需要采取更多措施与国家建立联系，更好地讲述我们在政府中所做的事情，同时也阐明我们希望国家走向何方的愿景。这就是安迪作为我们下一任首相能够提供的东西。他是一位出色的沟通者，他在大曼彻斯特有着出色的表现，我毫不怀疑他将把这一点带到首相的位置上。”

articles.title: 'We are with you, Venezuela': Houston community rallies after deadly earthquakes
articles.summary: Venezuelans living in the U.S organize donation drives in response to the devastating earthquakes.
articles.titleZh: “委内瑞拉，我们与你同在”：致命地震后休斯顿社区集会
articles.summaryZh: 居住在美国的委内瑞拉人组织捐款活动，以应对毁灭性的地震。

contentParagraphs.en: Messages of hope and support cover a wall beside a Venezuelan flag at MetaBox Cargo in Katy, Texas, on Thursday.
contentParagraphs.zh: 周四，德克萨斯州凯蒂 MetaBox Cargo 的委内瑞拉国旗旁的墙上写满了希望和支持的信息。

contentParagraphs.en: Lucio Vasquez/The Texas Newsroom
contentParagraphs.zh: 卢西奥·瓦斯奎兹/德克萨斯州新闻编辑室

contentParagraphs.en: Lucio Vasquez/The Texas Newsroom
contentParagraphs.zh: 卢西奥·瓦斯奎兹/德克萨斯州新闻编辑室

contentParagraphs.en: KATY, Texas — Arianna Regardia had just gotten to her mother's house when she saw the news Wednesday night. She saw apartment buildings reduced to heaps of concrete. She saw emergency crews digging through mountains of rubble, trying to reach people trapped deep within.
contentParagraphs.zh: 德克萨斯州凯蒂——星期三晚上，阿里安娜·雷加迪亚 (Arianna Regardia) 刚回到母亲家就看到了这个消息。她看到公寓楼变成了一堆混凝土。她看到急救人员在废墟堆中挖掘，试图救出被困在深处的人们。

contentParagraphs.en: She saw the devastation wrought by two powerful earthquakes that struck Venezuela's northern coast just hours earlier.
contentParagraphs.zh: 她亲眼目睹了几个小时前袭击委内瑞拉北部海岸的两次强烈地震所造成的破坏。

contentParagraphs.en: Regardia immediately called her grandmother, who lives in the capital of Caracas, near the epicenter of the quakes.
contentParagraphs.zh: 雷加迪亚立即给她的祖母打电话，她住在首都加拉加斯，靠近地震震中。

contentParagraphs.en: After a few seconds on the phone, she got an answer on the other side of the line. Her family was safe.
contentParagraphs.zh: 接通电话几秒钟后，电话那头就接到了接听电话。她的家人很安全。

contentParagraphs.en: But relief quickly gave way to a different feeling: the need to help.
contentParagraphs.zh: 但缓解很快就被一种不同的感觉所取代：需要帮助。

contentParagraphs.en: About 12 hours later, Regardia stood in a narrow cargo garage in this city about 30 minutes west of Houston, where bags of canned tuna, diapers and Advil lined the floor as volunteers sorted donations into cardboard boxes.
contentParagraphs.zh: 大约 12 小时后，Regardia 站在距离休斯顿以西约 30 分钟车程的这座城市的一个狭窄的货运车库里，地板上摆满了袋装金枪鱼罐头、尿布和止痛药，志愿者们将捐赠品分类装入纸板箱。

contentParagraphs.en: Regardia arrived Thursday morning to drop off hygiene items like pads, tampons and body soap. But shortly after, she was helping pack boxes as more and more donations came in.
contentParagraphs.zh: Regardia 于周四早上抵达，送来卫生巾、卫生棉条和沐浴露等卫生用品。但不久之后，随着越来越多的捐款涌入，她开始帮忙打包箱子。

contentParagraphs.en: "One thing I admire about being Venezuelan is our resilience," Regardia said, as tears welled in her eyes. "Being there for each other and just supporting each other during the tough times."
contentParagraphs.zh: “作为委内瑞拉人，我钦佩的一件事就是我们的韧性，”雷加迪亚说，泪水在眼眶里打转。 “在困难时期互相支持，互相支持。”

contentParagraphs.en: Regardia is one of roughly 83,000 Venezuelans living in the greater Houston area, home to one of the nation's largest Venezuelan communities, according to the Pew Research Center. She said it's been difficult to watch the disaster unfold from thousands of miles away.
contentParagraphs.zh: 根据皮尤研究中心的数据，雷加迪亚是居住在大休斯顿地区的大约 83,000 名委内瑞拉人之一，该地区是全国最大的委内瑞拉社区之一。她说，很难在千里之外观看灾难的发生。

contentParagraphs.en: "Being far away from your family, and being on the outside…it's very important for us to come together and support our people," Regardia said.
contentParagraphs.zh: 雷加迪亚说：“远离家人，身处局外……对我们来说，团结起来支持我们的人民非常重要。”

contentParagraphs.en: Arianna Regardia sorts donations at MetaBox Cargo in Katy, Texas, on Thursday. Regardia joined volunteers packing food, hygiene items and other essentials for earthquake survivors in Venezuela after learning her family back home was safe.
contentParagraphs.zh: 周四，阿里安娜·雷加迪亚 (Arianna Regardia) 在德克萨斯州凯蒂的 MetaBox Cargo 整理捐款。在得知家人安全后，雷加迪亚与志愿者一起为委内瑞拉地震幸存者打包食品、卫生用品和其他必需品。

contentParagraphs.en: Lucio Vasquez/The Texas Newsroom
contentParagraphs.zh: 卢西奥·瓦斯奎兹/德克萨斯州新闻编辑室

contentParagraphs.en: Lucio Vasquez/The Texas Newsroom
contentParagraphs.zh: 卢西奥·瓦斯奎兹/德克萨斯州新闻编辑室

contentParagraphs.en: The Wednesday evening quakes — a 7.2-magnitude foreshock followed less than a minute later by a 7.5-magnitude quake — killed at least 235 people and injured roughly 4,300 others, according to Venezuelan officials and the U.S. Geological Survey. Officials say hundreds of people may still be trapped under debris.
contentParagraphs.zh: 据委内瑞拉官员和美国地质调查局称，周三晚上的地震——7.2级前震，不到一分钟后又发生7.5级地震——造成至少235人死亡，约4,300人受伤。官员称，数百人可能仍被困在废墟下。

contentParagraphs.en: On Thursday, the Trump administration pledged $150 million for relief efforts.
contentParagraphs.zh: 周四，特朗普政府承诺提供 1.5 亿美元用于救灾工作。

contentParagraphs.en: The U.S. military, which has had a heightened presence in Venezuela since the January arrest of then-President Nicolás Maduro on drug charges, said that it will provide aircraft to help support search-and-rescue operations and deliver aid, according to the U.S. Department of State.
contentParagraphs.zh: 据美国国务院称，自一月份因毒品指控逮捕时任总统尼古拉斯·马杜罗以来，美国军方加强了在委内瑞拉的军事存在，并表示将提供飞机来帮助支持搜救行动并提供援助。

contentParagraphs.en: In response to the quakes, several donation sites opened across the Houston area as members of the city's Venezuelan community rushed to help from more than 2,000 miles away.
contentParagraphs.zh: 为了应对地震，休斯顿地区开设了多个捐款点，该市的委内瑞拉社区成员从 2000 多英里外赶来提供帮助。

contentParagraphs.en: Organizers said the supplies would be shipped to Venezuela as soon as possible, though damage to Caracas' main airport could complicate those efforts.
contentParagraphs.zh: 组织者表示，这些物资将尽快运往委内瑞拉，但加拉加斯主要机场的损坏可能会使这些努力变得复杂。

contentParagraphs.en: Similar donation efforts emerged across the U.S., where an estimated 1.2 million Venezuelans lived as of 2024, according to the Pew Research Center.
contentParagraphs.zh: 据皮尤研究中心称，截至 2024 年，美国各地也出现了类似的捐赠活动，估计有 120 万委内瑞拉人居住在美国。

contentParagraphs.en: "We are outside the country, the only way we can help is — our stuff. Clothes, food, something to send to Venezuela to help," said Alberto Avila, who dropped off three bags of canned food and clothing at Mi Querencia Latin Market, another donation site in Katy.
contentParagraphs.zh: 阿尔贝托·阿维拉说：“我们在国外，唯一能提供帮助的就是我们的东西。衣服、食物，还有一些可以寄到委内瑞拉去帮忙的东西。”他在凯蒂的另一个捐赠地点Mi Querencia Latin Market送去了三袋罐头食品和衣服。

contentParagraphs.en: Jesus Nunez, owner of MeetaBox Cargo in Katy, Texas, helps sort donated food, hygiene supplies and other essentials during a donation drive on Thursday.
contentParagraphs.zh: 德克萨斯州凯蒂 MeetaBox Cargo 的老板耶稣·努涅斯 (Jesus Nunez) 在周四的捐赠活动中帮助分类捐赠的食品、卫生用品和其他必需品。

contentParagraphs.en: Lucio Vasquez/The Texas Newsroom
contentParagraphs.zh: 卢西奥·瓦斯奎兹/德克萨斯州新闻编辑室

contentParagraphs.en: Lucio Vasquez/The Texas Newsroom
contentParagraphs.zh: 卢西奥·瓦斯奎兹/德克萨斯州新闻编辑室

contentParagraphs.en: Outside the market, volunteers dressed in the Venezuelan flag's yellow, blue and red greeted arriving donors. One woman quietly wiped away tears as more donations came in. By midafternoon, donation boxes were piling up inside along the market's aisles.
contentParagraphs.zh: 市场外，身穿委内瑞拉国旗黄、蓝、红衣服的志愿者迎接到来的捐赠者。随着越来越多的捐款进来，一名妇女悄悄地擦着眼泪。到了下午三点左右，捐款箱已经沿着市场的过道堆积起来。

contentParagraphs.en: "That's what Venezuelans do for our people," Avila said. He also feared for relatives in Venezuela, but later learned they were safe.
contentParagraphs.zh: “这就是委内瑞拉人为我们人民所做的事情，”阿维拉说。他还担心委内瑞拉的亲戚，但后来得知他们很安全。

contentParagraphs.en: Within the same hour, Amelia Millan handed bags of donations to volunteers. She said she's been in the U.S. for about 21 years, and like her compatriots Regardia and Avila, she worried for the safety of her family back in her home country.
contentParagraphs.zh: 在同一小时内，阿米莉亚·米兰将一袋袋的捐款交给了志愿者。她说，她已经在美国生活了大约21年，和她的同胞Regardia和Avila一样，她担心自己在祖国的家人的安全。

contentParagraphs.en: Thankfully, they were safe, though many of her family member's homes were badly damaged.
contentParagraphs.zh: 值得庆幸的是，尽管她的许多家人的房屋遭到严重损坏，但他们很安全。

contentParagraphs.en: "I wish I could get on a plane and be there right now, believe me, my heart and my mind are there right now," Millan said. "It's very hard to concentrate on work or anything else, but this is something else we can do."
contentParagraphs.zh: “我希望我现在就可以登上飞机到达那里，相信我，我的心和我的思想现在就在那里，”米兰说。 “很难专注于工作或其他事情，但这是我们可以做的其他事情。”

contentParagraphs.en: By late afternoon, the two donation sites buzzed with the sounds of ripped packing tape and conversations in Spanish. Inside the hot garage at MetaBox Cargo, between packing supplies, many paused to write messages of hope and support on the wall beside a Venezuelan flag.
contentParagraphs.zh: 到了下午晚些时候，两个捐赠地点就充斥着撕开包装胶带的声音和西班牙语的对话声。在 MetaBox Cargo 炎热的车库内，在包装物资的间隙，许多人停下来，在委内瑞拉国旗旁边的墙上写下了希望和支持的信息。

contentParagraphs.en: One message read: "Estamos contigo, Venezuela."
contentParagraphs.zh: 其中一条消息写道：“委内瑞拉，我们与你同在。”

contentParagraphs.en: We are with you, Venezuela.
contentParagraphs.zh: 委内瑞拉，我们与你同在。
